skill.name: Azure API Center Management SDK for Python
skill.description: A Python SDK for managing Azure API Center resources including APIs, versions, specifications, deployments, and workspaces. Enables AI assistants to programmatically interact with Azure API Center for API inventory management and governance.
skill.summary: Python SDK for Azure API Center resource management and API inventory governance
skill.author: sickn33

content.user_title: Manage Azure API Center with Python SDK
content.value_statement: Azure API Center provides a centralized repository for discovering and managing APIs across an organization. This skill enables AI assistants to interact with Azure API Center using the official Python SDK, allowing developers to automate API inventory management, governance, and lifecycle operations.
actual_capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete API resources in Azure API Center
Manage API versions and specifications including OpenAPI and other formats
Handle API deployments and workspace configurations
List and query APIs across multiple workspaces and collections
Work with API definitions and documentation metadata
limitations: Does not execute actual API calls - provides guidance and code generation only
Requires valid Azure credentials and subscription access to function
Cannot bypass Azure RBAC permissions or policies
Does not provide real-time API monitoring or analytics

use_cases.title: Generate SDK initialization code
use_cases.description: Create Python code to authenticate and initialize the Azure API Center client for a specific subscription and resource group.
use_cases.target_user: Python developers integrating Azure API Center

use_cases.title: Automate API inventory operations
use_cases.description: Generate scripts to bulk-create, update, or migrate API definitions across multiple workspaces or environments.

use_cases.title: Document API governance policies
use_cases.description: Create documentation and code examples for enforcing API standards, versioning strategies, and lifecycle management workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize API Center client
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to authenticate and create a client for Azure API Center using the Python SDK with service principal credentials.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Azure API Center SDK

prompt_templates.title: List all APIs in workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Python code to list all APIs registered in a specific workspace, including their versions and specifications.

prompt_templates.title: Create new API version
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate code to add a new API version with OpenAPI 3.0 specification to an existing API in Azure API Center.

prompt_templates.title: Manage API deployments
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to create and manage API deployments across different environments using the Azure API Center SDK.

output_examples.input: How do I create a new API in Azure API Center?
output_examples.output:
To create a new API in Azure API Center using the Python SDK:

1. First, authenticate using DefaultAzureCredential or ServicePrincipalCredential
2. Initialize the ApiCenterClient
3. Use the apis.create_or_update() method

Example:
```python
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.mgmt.apicenter import ApiCenterClient

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
client = ApiCenterClient(credential, subscription_id)

client.apis.create_or_update(
    resource_group_name='my-rg',
    service_name='my-api-center',
    workspace_name='default',
    api_name='my-new-api',
    api={'properties': {'title': 'My API', 'description': 'API description'}}
)
```

output_examples.input: What are the required permissions to manage APIs?
output_examples.output:
To manage APIs in Azure API Center, you need one of these roles at the appropriate scope:

- API Center API Manager: Full management access
- API Center Service Contributor: Create and update APIs
- API Center Reader: Read-only access
- Custom role with Microsoft.ApiCenter/*/permissions

The service principal or user identity must also have access to the Azure subscription containing the API Center.

best_practices: Use managed identities or service principals for production authentication rather than storing credentials in code
Implement retry policies with exponential backoff when calling Azure SDK methods to handle transient failures
Organize APIs by workspace to separate development, staging, and production API catalogs
anti_patterns: Hardcoding Azure credentials in source code or configuration files - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault instead
Ignoring API versioning - always create new versions rather than overwriting existing ones to maintain compatibility
Granting overly broad permissions to the SDK client - follow principle of least privilege for RBAC roles

faq.question: What authentication methods does this SDK support?
faq.answer: The SDK supports Azure Active Directory authentication including managed identities, service principals, and user credentials. Use DefaultAzureCredential for automatic credential resolution in Azure environments.

faq.question: Can this skill help with migrating APIs from other platforms?
faq.answer: This skill can generate code to import API definitions from OpenAPI, Swagger, or other formats into Azure API Center. However, the actual migration requires valid source API specifications and Azure API Center access.

faq.question: Does this skill support Azure API Center v2?
faq.answer: Yes, this SDK supports the latest Azure API Center service. Check the Azure SDK release notes for version-specific features and breaking changes.

faq.question: How do I handle rate limiting with the API Center SDK?
faq.answer: Implement exponential backoff retry policies. The Azure SDK does not include built-in rate limiting handling, so use libraries like tenacity or implement custom retry logic.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: The skill provides documentation and code generation guidance. It can generate example code offline but cannot make actual Azure API calls without network connectivity.

faq.question: What Azure regions support API Center?
faq.answer: Azure API Center is available in most Azure regions. Check the Azure portal or documentation for the latest regional availability.
